contrib/pg_stat_statements/pg_stat_statements.c
1/*-------------------------------------------------------------------------
2 *
3 * pg_stat_statements.c
4 *		Track statement planning and execution times as well as resource
5 *		usage across a whole database cluster.
6 *
7 * Execution costs are totaled for each distinct source query, and kept in
8 * a shared hashtable.  (We track only as many distinct queries as will fit
9 * in the designated amount of shared memory.)
10 *
11 * Starting in Postgres 9.2, this module normalized query entries.  As of
12 * Postgres 14, the normalization is done by the core if compute_query_id is
13 * enabled, or optionally by third-party modules.
14 *
15 * To facilitate presenting entries to users, we create "representative" query
16 * strings in which constants are replaced with parameter symbols ($n), to
17 * make it clearer what a normalized entry can represent.  To save on shared
18 * memory, and to avoid having to truncate oversized query strings, we store
19 * these strings in a temporary external query-texts file.  Offsets into this
20 * file are kept in shared memory.
21 *
22 * Note about locking issues: to create or delete an entry in the shared
23 * hashtable, one must hold pgss->lock exclusively.  Modifying any field
24 * in an entry except the counters requires the same.  To look up an entry,
25 * one must hold the lock shared.  To read or update the counters within
26 * an entry, one must hold the lock shared or exclusive (so the entry doesn't
27 * disappear!) and also take the entry's mutex spinlock.
28 * The shared state variable pgss->extent (the next free spot in the external
29 * query-text file) should be accessed only while holding either the
30 * pgss->mutex spinlock, or exclusive lock on pgss->lock.  We use the mutex to
31 * allow reserving file space while holding only shared lock on pgss->lock.
32 * Rewriting the entire external query-text file, eg for garbage collection,
33 * requires holding pgss->lock exclusively; this allows individual entries
34 * in the file to be read or written while holding only shared lock.
35 *
36 *
37 * Copyright (c) 2008-2025, PostgreSQL Global Development Group
38 *
39 * IDENTIFICATION
40 *	  contrib/pg_stat_statements/pg_stat_statements.c
41 *
42 *-------------------------------------------------------------------------
43 */
44#include "postgres.h"
45
46#include <math.h>
47#include <sys/stat.h>
48#include <unistd.h>
49
50#include "access/parallel.h"
51#include "catalog/pg_authid.h"
52#include "common/int.h"
53#include "executor/instrument.h"
54#include "funcapi.h"
55#include "jit/jit.h"
56#include "mb/pg_wchar.h"
57#include "miscadmin.h"
58#include "nodes/queryjumble.h"
59#include "optimizer/planner.h"
60#include "parser/analyze.h"
61#include "parser/scanner.h"
62#include "pgstat.h"
63#include "storage/fd.h"
64#include "storage/ipc.h"
65#include "storage/lwlock.h"
66#include "storage/shmem.h"
67#include "storage/spin.h"
68#include "tcop/utility.h"
69#include "utils/acl.h"
70#include "utils/builtins.h"
71#include "utils/memutils.h"
72#include "utils/timestamp.h"
73
74PG_MODULE_MAGIC_EXT(
75					.name = "pg_stat_statements",
76					.version = PG_VERSION
77);
78
79/* Location of permanent stats file (valid when database is shut down) */ view japanese
80#define PGSS_DUMP_FILE	PGSTAT_STAT_PERMANENT_DIRECTORY "/pg_stat_statements.stat"
81
82/*
83 * Location of external query text file.
84 */ view japanese
85#define PGSS_TEXT_FILE	PG_STAT_TMP_DIR "/pgss_query_texts.stat"
86
87/* Magic number identifying the stats file format */ view japanese
88static const uint32 PGSS_FILE_HEADER = 0x20220408;
89
90/* PostgreSQL major version number, changes in which invalidate all entries */ view japanese
91static const uint32 PGSS_PG_MAJOR_VERSION = PG_VERSION_NUM / 100;
92
93/* XXX: Should USAGE_EXEC reflect execution time and/or buffer usage? */ view japanese
94#define USAGE_EXEC(duration)	(1.0)
95#define USAGE_INIT				(1.0)	/* including initial planning */ view japanese
96#define ASSUMED_MEDIAN_INIT		(10.0)	/* initial assumed median usage */ view japanese
97#define ASSUMED_LENGTH_INIT		1024	/* initial assumed mean query length */ view japanese
98#define USAGE_DECREASE_FACTOR	(0.99)	/* decreased every entry_dealloc */ view japanese
99#define STICKY_DECREASE_FACTOR	(0.50)	/* factor for sticky entries */ view japanese
100#define USAGE_DEALLOC_PERCENT	5	/* free this % of entries at once */ view japanese
101#define IS_STICKY(c)	((c.calls[PGSS_PLAN] + c.calls[PGSS_EXEC]) == 0)
102
103/*
104 * Extension version number, for supporting older extension versions' objects
105 */ view japanese
106typedef enum pgssVersion
107{
108	PGSS_V1_0 = 0,
109	PGSS_V1_1,
110	PGSS_V1_2,
111	PGSS_V1_3,
112	PGSS_V1_8,
113	PGSS_V1_9,
114	PGSS_V1_10,
115	PGSS_V1_11,
116	PGSS_V1_12,
117} pgssVersion;
118
119typedef enum pgssStoreKind
120{
121	PGSS_INVALID = -1,
122
123	/*
124	 * PGSS_PLAN and PGSS_EXEC must be respectively 0 and 1 as they're used to
125	 * reference the underlying values in the arrays in the Counters struct,
126	 * and this order is required in pg_stat_statements_internal().
127	 */ view japanese
128	PGSS_PLAN = 0,
129	PGSS_EXEC,
130} pgssStoreKind;
131
132#define PGSS_NUMKIND (PGSS_EXEC + 1)
133
134/*
135 * Hashtable key that defines the identity of a hashtable entry.  We separate
136 * queries by user and by database even if they are otherwise identical.
137 *
138 * If you add a new key to this struct, make sure to teach pgss_store() to
139 * zero the padding bytes.  Otherwise, things will break, because pgss_hash is
140 * created using HASH_BLOBS, and thus tag_hash is used to hash this.
141
142 */ view japanese
143typedef struct pgssHashKey
144{
145	Oid			userid;			/* user OID */ view japanese
146	Oid			dbid;			/* database OID */ view japanese
147	int64		queryid;		/* query identifier */ view japanese
148	bool		toplevel;		/* query executed at top level */ view japanese
149} pgssHashKey;
150
151/*
152 * The actual stats counters kept within pgssEntry.
153 */ view japanese
154typedef struct Counters
155{
156	int64		calls[PGSS_NUMKIND];	/* # of times planned/executed */ view japanese
157	double		total_time[PGSS_NUMKIND];	/* total planning/execution time,
158											 * in msec */ view japanese
159	double		min_time[PGSS_NUMKIND]; /* minimum planning/execution time in
160										 * msec since min/max reset */ view japanese
161	double		max_time[PGSS_NUMKIND]; /* maximum planning/execution time in
162										 * msec since min/max reset */ view japanese
163	double		mean_time[PGSS_NUMKIND];	/* mean planning/execution time in
164											 * msec */ view japanese
165	double		sum_var_time[PGSS_NUMKIND]; /* sum of variances in
166											 * planning/execution time in msec */ view japanese
167	int64		rows;			/* total # of retrieved or affected rows */ view japanese
168	int64		shared_blks_hit;	/* # of shared buffer hits */ view japanese
169	int64		shared_blks_read;	/* # of shared disk blocks read */ view japanese
170	int64		shared_blks_dirtied;	/* # of shared disk blocks dirtied */ view japanese
171	int64		shared_blks_written;	/* # of shared disk blocks written */ view japanese
172	int64		local_blks_hit; /* # of local buffer hits */ view japanese
173	int64		local_blks_read;	/* # of local disk blocks read */ view japanese
174	int64		local_blks_dirtied; /* # of local disk blocks dirtied */ view japanese
175	int64		local_blks_written; /* # of local disk blocks written */ view japanese
176	int64		temp_blks_read; /* # of temp blocks read */ view japanese
177	int64		temp_blks_written;	/* # of temp blocks written */ view japanese
178	double		shared_blk_read_time;	/* time spent reading shared blocks,
179										 * in msec */ view japanese
180	double		shared_blk_write_time;	/* time spent writing shared blocks,
181										 * in msec */ view japanese
182	double		local_blk_read_time;	/* time spent reading local blocks, in
183										 * msec */ view japanese
184	double		local_blk_write_time;	/* time spent writing local blocks, in
185										 * msec */ view japanese
186	double		temp_blk_read_time; /* time spent reading temp blocks, in msec */ view japanese
187	double		temp_blk_write_time;	/* time spent writing temp blocks, in
188										 * msec */ view japanese
189	double		usage;			/* usage factor */ view japanese
190	int64		wal_records;	/* # of WAL records generated */ view japanese
191	int64		wal_fpi;		/* # of WAL full page images generated */ view japanese
192	uint64		wal_bytes;		/* total amount of WAL generated in bytes */ view japanese
193	int64		wal_buffers_full;	/* # of times the WAL buffers became full */ view japanese
194	int64		jit_functions;	/* total number of JIT functions emitted */ view japanese
195	double		jit_generation_time;	/* total time to generate jit code */ view japanese
196	int64		jit_inlining_count; /* number of times inlining time has been
197									 * > 0 */ view japanese
198	double		jit_deform_time;	/* total time to deform tuples in jit code */ view japanese
199	int64		jit_deform_count;	/* number of times deform time has been >
200									 * 0 */ view japanese
201
202	double		jit_inlining_time;	/* total time to inline jit code */ view japanese
203	int64		jit_optimization_count; /* number of times optimization time
204										 * has been > 0 */ view japanese
205	double		jit_optimization_time;	/* total time to optimize jit code */ view japanese
206	int64		jit_emission_count; /* number of times emission time has been
207									 * > 0 */ view japanese
208	double		jit_emission_time;	/* total time to emit jit code */ view japanese
209	int64		parallel_workers_to_launch; /* # of parallel workers planned
210											 * to be launched */ view japanese
211	int64		parallel_workers_launched;	/* # of parallel workers actually
212											 * launched */ view japanese
213} Counters;
214
215/*
216 * Global statistics for pg_stat_statements
217 */ view japanese
218typedef struct pgssGlobalStats
219{
220	int64		dealloc;		/* # of times entries were deallocated */ view japanese
221	TimestampTz stats_reset;	/* timestamp with all stats reset */ view japanese
222} pgssGlobalStats;
223
224/*
225 * Statistics per statement
226 *
227 * Note: in event of a failure in garbage collection of the query text file,
228 * we reset query_offset to zero and query_len to -1.  This will be seen as
229 * an invalid state by qtext_fetch().
230 */ view japanese
231typedef struct pgssEntry
232{
233	pgssHashKey key;			/* hash key of entry - MUST BE FIRST */ view japanese
234	Counters	counters;		/* the statistics for this query */ view japanese
235	Size		query_offset;	/* query text offset in external file */ view japanese
236	int			query_len;		/* # of valid bytes in query string, or -1 */ view japanese
237	int			encoding;		/* query text encoding */ view japanese
238	TimestampTz stats_since;	/* timestamp of entry allocation */ view japanese
239	TimestampTz minmax_stats_since; /* timestamp of last min/max values reset */ view japanese
240	slock_t		mutex;			/* protects the counters only */ view japanese
241} pgssEntry;
242
243/*
244 * Global shared state
245 */ view japanese
246typedef struct pgssSharedState
247{
248	LWLock	   *lock;			/* protects hashtable search/modification */ view japanese
249	double		cur_median_usage;	/* current median usage in hashtable */ view japanese
250	Size		mean_query_len; /* current mean entry text length */ view japanese
251	slock_t		mutex;			/* protects following fields only: */ view japanese
252	Size		extent;			/* current extent of query file */ view japanese
253	int			n_writers;		/* number of active writers to query file */ view japanese
254	int			gc_count;		/* query file garbage collection cycle count */ view japanese
255	pgssGlobalStats stats;		/* global statistics for pgss */ view japanese
256} pgssSharedState;
257
258/*---- Local variables ----*/ view japanese
259
260/* Current nesting depth of planner/ExecutorRun/ProcessUtility calls */ view japanese
261static int	nesting_level = 0;
262
263/* Saved hook values */ view japanese
264static shmem_request_hook_type prev_shmem_request_hook = NULL;
265static shmem_startup_hook_type prev_shmem_startup_hook = NULL;
266static post_parse_analyze_hook_type prev_post_parse_analyze_hook = NULL;
267static planner_hook_type prev_planner_hook = NULL;
268static ExecutorStart_hook_type prev_ExecutorStart = NULL;
269static ExecutorRun_hook_type prev_ExecutorRun = NULL;
270static ExecutorFinish_hook_type prev_ExecutorFinish = NULL;
271static ExecutorEnd_hook_type prev_ExecutorEnd = NULL;
272static ProcessUtility_hook_type prev_ProcessUtility = NULL;
273
274/* Links to shared memory state */ view japanese
275static pgssSharedState *pgss = NULL;
276static HTAB *pgss_hash = NULL;
277
278/*---- GUC variables ----*/ view japanese
279
280typedef enum
281{
282	PGSS_TRACK_NONE,			/* track no statements */ view japanese
283	PGSS_TRACK_TOP,				/* only top level statements */ view japanese
284	PGSS_TRACK_ALL,				/* all statements, including nested ones */ view japanese
285}			PGSSTrackLevel;
286
287static const struct config_enum_entry track_options[] =
288{
289	{"none", PGSS_TRACK_NONE, false},
290	{"top", PGSS_TRACK_TOP, false},
291	{"all", PGSS_TRACK_ALL, false},
292	{NULL, 0, false}
293};
294
295static int	pgss_max = 5000;	/* max # statements to track */ view japanese
296static int	pgss_track = PGSS_TRACK_TOP;	/* tracking level */ view japanese
297static bool pgss_track_utility = true;	/* whether to track utility commands */ view japanese
298static bool pgss_track_planning = false;	/* whether to track planning
299											 * duration */ view japanese
300static bool pgss_save = true;	/* whether to save stats across shutdown */ view japanese
301
302#define pgss_enabled(level) \
303	(!IsParallelWorker() && \
304	(pgss_track == PGSS_TRACK_ALL || \
305	(pgss_track == PGSS_TRACK_TOP && (level) == 0)))
306
307#define record_gc_qtexts() \
308	do { \
309		SpinLockAcquire(&pgss->mutex); \
310		pgss->gc_count++; \
311		SpinLockRelease(&pgss->mutex); \
312	} while(0)
313
314/*---- Function declarations ----*/ view japanese
315
316PG_FUNCTION_INFO_V1(pg_stat_statements_reset);
317PG_FUNCTION_INFO_V1(pg_stat_statements_reset_1_7);
318PG_FUNCTION_INFO_V1(pg_stat_statements_reset_1_11);
319PG_FUNCTION_INFO_V1(pg_stat_statements_1_2);
320PG_FUNCTION_INFO_V1(pg_stat_statements_1_3);
321PG_FUNCTION_INFO_V1(pg_stat_statements_1_8);
322PG_FUNCTION_INFO_V1(pg_stat_statements_1_9);
323PG_FUNCTION_INFO_V1(pg_stat_statements_1_10);
324PG_FUNCTION_INFO_V1(pg_stat_statements_1_11);
325PG_FUNCTION_INFO_V1(pg_stat_statements_1_12);
326PG_FUNCTION_INFO_V1(pg_stat_statements);
327PG_FUNCTION_INFO_V1(pg_stat_statements_info);
328
329static void pgss_shmem_request(void);
330static void pgss_shmem_startup(void);
331static void pgss_shmem_shutdown(int code, Datum arg);
332static void pgss_post_parse_analyze(ParseState *pstate, Query *query,
333									JumbleState *jstate);
334static PlannedStmt *pgss_planner(Query *parse,
335								 const char *query_string,
336								 int cursorOptions,
337								 ParamListInfo boundParams);
338static void pgss_ExecutorStart(QueryDesc *queryDesc, int eflags);
339static void pgss_ExecutorRun(QueryDesc *queryDesc,
340							 ScanDirection direction,
341							 uint64 count);
342static void pgss_ExecutorFinish(QueryDesc *queryDesc);
343static void pgss_ExecutorEnd(QueryDesc *queryDesc);
344static void pgss_ProcessUtility(PlannedStmt *pstmt, const char *queryString,
345								bool readOnlyTree,
346								ProcessUtilityContext context, ParamListInfo params,
347								QueryEnvironment *queryEnv,
348								DestReceiver *dest, QueryCompletion *qc);
349static void pgss_store(const char *query, int64 queryId,
350					   int query_location, int query_len,
351					   pgssStoreKind kind,
352					   double total_time, uint64 rows,
353					   const BufferUsage *bufusage,
354					   const WalUsage *walusage,
355					   const struct JitInstrumentation *jitusage,
356					   JumbleState *jstate,
357					   int parallel_workers_to_launch,
358					   int parallel_workers_launched);
359static void pg_stat_statements_internal(FunctionCallInfo fcinfo,
360										pgssVersion api_version,
361										bool showtext);
362static Size pgss_memsize(void);
363static pgssEntry *entry_alloc(pgssHashKey *key, Size query_offset, int query_len,
364							  int encoding, bool sticky);
365static void entry_dealloc(void);
366static bool qtext_store(const char *query, int query_len,
367						Size *query_offset, int *gc_count);
368static char *qtext_load_file(Size *buffer_size);
369static char *qtext_fetch(Size query_offset, int query_len,
370						 char *buffer, Size buffer_size);
371static bool need_gc_qtexts(void);
372static void gc_qtexts(void);
373static TimestampTz entry_reset(Oid userid, Oid dbid, int64 queryid, bool minmax_only);
374static char *generate_normalized_query(JumbleState *jstate, const char *query,
375									   int query_loc, int *query_len_p);
376static void fill_in_constant_lengths(JumbleState *jstate, const char *query,
377									 int query_loc);
378static int	comp_location(const void *a, const void *b);
379
380
381/*
382 * Module load callback
383 */ view japanese
384void
385_PG_init(void)
386{
387	/*
388	 * In order to create our shared memory area, we have to be loaded via
389	 * shared_preload_libraries.  If not, fall out without hooking into any of
390	 * the main system.  (We don't throw error here because it seems useful to
391	 * allow the pg_stat_statements functions to be created even when the
392	 * module isn't active.  The functions must protect themselves against
393	 * being called then, however.)
394	 */ view japanese
395	if (!process_shared_preload_libraries_in_progress)
396		return;
397
398	/*
399	 * Inform the postmaster that we want to enable query_id calculation if
400	 * compute_query_id is set to auto.
401	 */ view japanese
402	EnableQueryId();
403
404	/*
405	 * Define (or redefine) custom GUC variables.
406	 */ view japanese
407	DefineCustomIntVariable("pg_stat_statements.max",
408							"Sets the maximum number of statements tracked by pg_stat_statements.",
409							NULL,
410							&pgss_max,
411							5000,
412							100,
413							INT_MAX / 2,
414							PGC_POSTMASTER,
415							0,
416							NULL,
417							NULL,
418							NULL);
419
420	DefineCustomEnumVariable("pg_stat_statements.track",
421							 "Selects which statements are tracked by pg_stat_statements.",
422							 NULL,
423							 &pgss_track,
424							 PGSS_TRACK_TOP,
425							 track_options,
426							 PGC_SUSET,
427							 0,
428							 NULL,
429							 NULL,
430							 NULL);
431
432	DefineCustomBoolVariable("pg_stat_statements.track_utility",
433							 "Selects whether utility commands are tracked by pg_stat_statements.",
434							 NULL,
435							 &pgss_track_utility,
436							 true,
437							 PGC_SUSET,
438							 0,
439							 NULL,
440							 NULL,
441							 NULL);
442
443	DefineCustomBoolVariable("pg_stat_statements.track_planning",
444							 "Selects whether planning duration is tracked by pg_stat_statements.",
445							 NULL,
446							 &pgss_track_planning,
447							 false,
448							 PGC_SUSET,
449							 0,
450							 NULL,
451							 NULL,
452							 NULL);
453
454	DefineCustomBoolVariable("pg_stat_statements.save",
455							 "Save pg_stat_statements statistics across server shutdowns.",
456							 NULL,
457							 &pgss_save,
458							 true,
459							 PGC_SIGHUP,
460							 0,
461							 NULL,
462							 NULL,
463							 NULL);
464
465	MarkGUCPrefixReserved("pg_stat_statements");
466
467	/*
468	 * Install hooks.
469	 */ view japanese
470	prev_shmem_request_hook = shmem_request_hook;
471	shmem_request_hook = pgss_shmem_request;
472	prev_shmem_startup_hook = shmem_startup_hook;
473	shmem_startup_hook = pgss_shmem_startup;
474	prev_post_parse_analyze_hook = post_parse_analyze_hook;
475	post_parse_analyze_hook = pgss_post_parse_analyze;
476	prev_planner_hook = planner_hook;
477	planner_hook = pgss_planner;
478	prev_ExecutorStart = ExecutorStart_hook;
479	ExecutorStart_hook = pgss_ExecutorStart;
480	prev_ExecutorRun = ExecutorRun_hook;
481	ExecutorRun_hook = pgss_ExecutorRun;
482	prev_ExecutorFinish = ExecutorFinish_hook;
483	ExecutorFinish_hook = pgss_ExecutorFinish;
484	prev_ExecutorEnd = ExecutorEnd_hook;
485	ExecutorEnd_hook = pgss_ExecutorEnd;
486	prev_ProcessUtility = ProcessUtility_hook;
487	ProcessUtility_hook = pgss_ProcessUtility;
488}
489
490/*
491 * shmem_request hook: request additional shared resources.  We'll allocate or
492 * attach to the shared resources in pgss_shmem_startup().
493 */ view japanese
494static void
495pgss_shmem_request(void)
496{
497	if (prev_shmem_request_hook)
498		prev_shmem_request_hook();
499
500	RequestAddinShmemSpace(pgss_memsize());
501	RequestNamedLWLockTranche("pg_stat_statements", 1);
502}
503
504/*
505 * shmem_startup hook: allocate or attach to shared memory,
506 * then load any pre-existing statistics from file.
507 * Also create and load the query-texts file, which is expected to exist
508 * (even if empty) while the module is enabled.
509 */ view japanese
510static void
511pgss_shmem_startup(void)
512{
513	bool		found;
514	HASHCTL		info;
515	FILE	   *file = NULL;
516	FILE	   *qfile = NULL;
517	uint32		header;
518	int32		num;
519	int32		pgver;
520	int32		i;
521	int			buffer_size;
522	char	   *buffer = NULL;
523
524	if (prev_shmem_startup_hook)
525		prev_shmem_startup_hook();
526
527	/* reset in case this is a restart within the postmaster */ view japanese
528	pgss = NULL;
529	pgss_hash = NULL;
530
531	/*
532	 * Create or attach to the shared memory state, including hash table
533	 */ view japanese
534	LWLockAcquire(AddinShmemInitLock, LW_EXCLUSIVE);
535
536	pgss = ShmemInitStruct("pg_stat_statements",
537						   sizeof(pgssSharedState),
538						   &found);
539
540	if (!found)
541	{
542		/* First time through ... */ view japanese
543		pgss->lock = &(GetNamedLWLockTranche("pg_stat_statements"))->lock;
544		pgss->cur_median_usage = ASSUMED_MEDIAN_INIT;
545		pgss->mean_query_len = ASSUMED_LENGTH_INIT;
546		SpinLockInit(&pgss->mutex);
547		pgss->extent = 0;
548		pgss->n_writers = 0;
549		pgss->gc_count = 0;
550		pgss->stats.dealloc = 0;
551		pgss->stats.stats_reset = GetCurrentTimestamp();
552	}
553
554	info.keysize = sizeof(pgssHashKey);
555	info.entrysize = sizeof(pgssEntry);
556	pgss_hash = ShmemInitHash("pg_stat_statements hash",
557							  pgss_max, pgss_max,
558							  &info,
559							  HASH_ELEM | HASH_BLOBS);
560
561	LWLockRelease(AddinShmemInitLock);
562
563	/*
564	 * If we're in the postmaster (or a standalone backend...), set up a shmem
565	 * exit hook to dump the statistics to disk.
566	 */ view japanese
567	if (!IsUnderPostmaster)
568		on_shmem_exit(pgss_shmem_shutdown, (Datum) 0);
569
570	/*
571	 * Done if some other process already completed our initialization.
572	 */ view japanese
573	if (found)
574		return;
575
576	/*
577	 * Note: we don't bother with locks here, because there should be no other
578	 * processes running when this code is reached.
579	 */ view japanese
580
581	/* Unlink query text file possibly left over from crash */ view japanese
582	unlink(PGSS_TEXT_FILE);
583
584	/* Allocate new query text temp file */ view japanese
585	qfile = AllocateFile(PGSS_TEXT_FILE, PG_BINARY_W);
586	if (qfile == NULL)
587		goto write_error;
588
589	/*
590	 * If we were told not to load old statistics, we're done.  (Note we do
591	 * not try to unlink any old dump file in this case.  This seems a bit
592	 * questionable but it's the historical behavior.)
593	 */ view japanese
594	if (!pgss_save)
595	{
596		FreeFile(qfile);
597		return;
598	}
599
600	/*
601	 * Attempt to load old statistics from the dump file.
602	 */ view japanese
603	file = AllocateFile(PGSS_DUMP_FILE, PG_BINARY_R);
604	if (file == NULL)
605	{
606		if (errno != ENOENT)
607			goto read_error;
608		/* No existing persisted stats file, so we're done */ view japanese
609		FreeFile(qfile);
610		return;
611	}
612
613	buffer_size = 2048;
614	buffer = (char *) palloc(buffer_size);
615
616	if (fread(&header, sizeof(uint32), 1, file) != 1 ||
617		fread(&pgver, sizeof(uint32), 1, file) != 1 ||
618		fread(&num, sizeof(int32), 1, file) != 1)
619		goto read_error;
620
621	if (header != PGSS_FILE_HEADER ||
622		pgver != PGSS_PG_MAJOR_VERSION)
623		goto data_error;
624
625	for (i = 0; i < num; i++)
626	{
627		pgssEntry	temp;
628		pgssEntry  *entry;
629		Size		query_offset;
630
631		if (fread(&temp, sizeof(pgssEntry), 1, file) != 1)
632			goto read_error;
633
634		/* Encoding is the only field we can easily sanity-check */ view japanese
635		if (!PG_VALID_BE_ENCODING(temp.encoding))
636			goto data_error;
637
638		/* Resize buffer as needed */ view japanese
639		if (temp.query_len >= buffer_size)
640		{
641			buffer_size = Max(buffer_size * 2, temp.query_len + 1);
642			buffer = repalloc(buffer, buffer_size);
643		}
644
645		if (fread(buffer, 1, temp.query_len + 1, file) != temp.query_len + 1)
646			goto read_error;
647
648		/* Should have a trailing null, but let's make sure */ view japanese
649		buffer[temp.query_len] = '\0';
650
651		/* Skip loading "sticky" entries */ view japanese
652		if (IS_STICKY(temp.counters))
653			continue;
654
655		/* Store the query text */ view japanese
656		query_offset = pgss->extent;
657		if (fwrite(buffer, 1, temp.query_len + 1, qfile) != temp.query_len + 1)
658			goto write_error;
659		pgss->extent += temp.query_len + 1;
660
661		/* make the hashtable entry (discards old entries if too many) */ view japanese
662		entry = entry_alloc(&temp.key, query_offset, temp.query_len,
663							temp.encoding,
664							false);
665
666		/* copy in the actual stats */ view japanese
667		entry->counters = temp.counters;
668		entry->stats_since = temp.stats_since;
669		entry->minmax_stats_since = temp.minmax_stats_since;
670	}
671
672	/* Read global statistics for pg_stat_statements */
673	if (fread(&pgss->stats, sizeof(pgssGlobalStats), 1, file) != 1)
674		goto read_error;
675
676	pfree(buffer);
677	FreeFile(file);
678	FreeFile(qfile);
679
680	/*
681	 * Remove the persisted stats file so it's not included in
682	 * backups/replication standbys, etc.  A new file will be written on next
683	 * shutdown.
684	 *
685	 * Note: it's okay if the PGSS_TEXT_FILE is included in a basebackup,
686	 * because we remove that file on startup; it acts inversely to
687	 * PGSS_DUMP_FILE, in that it is only supposed to be around when the
688	 * server is running, whereas PGSS_DUMP_FILE is only supposed to be around
689	 * when the server is not running.  Leaving the file creates no danger of
690	 * a newly restored database having a spurious record of execution costs,
691	 * which is what we're really concerned about here.
692	 */ view japanese
693	unlink(PGSS_DUMP_FILE);
694
695	return;
696
697read_error:
698	ereport(LOG,
699			(errcode_for_file_access(),
700			 errmsg("could not read file \"%s\": %m",
701					PGSS_DUMP_FILE)));
702	goto fail;
703data_error:
704	ereport(LOG,
705			(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),
706			 errmsg("ignoring invalid data in file \"%s\"",
707					PGSS_DUMP_FILE)));
708	goto fail;
709write_error:
710	ereport(LOG,
711			(errcode_for_file_access(),
712			 errmsg("could not write file \"%s\": %m",
713					PGSS_TEXT_FILE)));
714fail:
715	if (buffer)
716		pfree(buffer);
717	if (file)
718		FreeFile(file);
719	if (qfile)
720		FreeFile(qfile);
721	/* If possible, throw away the bogus file; ignore any error */ view japanese
722	unlink(PGSS_DUMP_FILE);
723
724	/*
725	 * Don't unlink PGSS_TEXT_FILE here; it should always be around while the
726	 * server is running with pg_stat_statements enabled
727	 */ view japanese
728}
729
730/*
731 * shmem_shutdown hook: Dump statistics into file.
732 *
733 * Note: we don't bother with acquiring lock, because there should be no
734 * other processes running when this is called.
735 */ view japanese
736static void
737pgss_shmem_shutdown(int code, Datum arg)
738{
739	FILE	   *file;
740	char	   *qbuffer = NULL;
741	Size		qbuffer_size = 0;
742	HASH_SEQ_STATUS hash_seq;
743	int32		num_entries;
744	pgssEntry  *entry;
745
746	/* Don't try to dump during a crash. */ view japanese
747	if (code)
748		return;
749
750	/* Safety check ... shouldn't get here unless shmem is set up. */ view japanese
751	if (!pgss || !pgss_hash)
752		return;
753
754	/* Don't dump if told not to. */ view japanese
755	if (!pgss_save)
756		return;
757
758	file = AllocateFile(PGSS_DUMP_FILE ".tmp", PG_BINARY_W);
759	if (file == NULL)
760		goto error;
761
762	if (fwrite(&PGSS_FILE_HEADER, sizeof(uint32), 1, file) != 1)
763		goto error;
764	if (fwrite(&PGSS_PG_MAJOR_VERSION, sizeof(uint32), 1, file) != 1)
765		goto error;
766	num_entries = hash_get_num_entries(pgss_hash);
767	if (fwrite(&num_entries, sizeof(int32), 1, file) != 1)
768		goto error;
769
770	qbuffer = qtext_load_file(&qbuffer_size);
771	if (qbuffer == NULL)
772		goto error;
773
774	/*
775	 * When serializing to disk, we store query texts immediately after their
776	 * entry data.  Any orphaned query texts are thereby excluded.
777	 */ view japanese
778	hash_seq_init(&hash_seq, pgss_hash);
779	while ((entry = hash_seq_search(&hash_seq)) != NULL)
780	{
781		int			len = entry->query_len;
782		char	   *qstr = qtext_fetch(entry->query_offset, len,
783									   qbuffer, qbuffer_size);
784
785		if (qstr == NULL)
786			continue;			/* Ignore any entries with bogus texts */ view japanese
787
788		if (fwrite(entry, sizeof(pgssEntry), 1, file) != 1 ||
789			fwrite(qstr, 1, len + 1, file) != len + 1)
790		{
791			/* note: we assume hash_seq_term won't change errno */ view japanese
792			hash_seq_term(&hash_seq);
793			goto error;
794		}
795	}
796
797	/* Dump global statistics for pg_stat_statements */ view japanese
798	if (fwrite(&pgss->stats, sizeof(pgssGlobalStats), 1, file) != 1)
799		goto error;
800
801	pfree(qbuffer);
802	qbuffer = NULL;
803
804	if (FreeFile(file))
805	{
806		file = NULL;
807		goto error;
808	}
809
810	/*
811	 * Rename file into place, so we atomically replace any old one.
812	 */ view japanese
813	(void) durable_rename(PGSS_DUMP_FILE ".tmp", PGSS_DUMP_FILE, LOG);
814
815	/* Unlink query-texts file; it's not needed while shutdown */ view japanese
816	unlink(PGSS_TEXT_FILE);
817
818	return;
819
820error:
821	ereport(LOG,
822			(errcode_for_file_access(),
823			 errmsg("could not write file \"%s\": %m",
824					PGSS_DUMP_FILE ".tmp")));
825	if (qbuffer)
826		pfree(qbuffer);
827	if (file)
828		FreeFile(file);
829	unlink(PGSS_DUMP_FILE ".tmp");
830	unlink(PGSS_TEXT_FILE);
831}
832
833/*
834 * Post-parse-analysis hook: mark query with a queryId
835 */ view japanese
836static void
837pgss_post_parse_analyze(ParseState *pstate, Query *query, JumbleState *jstate)
838{
839	if (prev_post_parse_analyze_hook)
840		prev_post_parse_analyze_hook(pstate, query, jstate);
841
842	/* Safety check... */
843	if (!pgss || !pgss_hash || !pgss_enabled(nesting_level))
844		return;
845
846	/*
847	 * If it's EXECUTE, clear the queryId so that stats will accumulate for
848	 * the underlying PREPARE.  But don't do this if we're not tracking
849	 * utility statements, to avoid messing up another extension that might be
850	 * tracking them.
851	 */ view japanese
852	if (query->utilityStmt)
853	{
854		if (pgss_track_utility && IsA(query->utilityStmt, ExecuteStmt))
855		{
856			query->queryId = INT64CONST(0);
857			return;
858		}
859	}
860
861	/*
862	 * If query jumbling were able to identify any ignorable constants, we
863	 * immediately create a hash table entry for the query, so that we can
864	 * record the normalized form of the query string.  If there were no such
865	 * constants, the normalized string would be the same as the query text
866	 * anyway, so there's no need for an early entry.
867	 */ view japanese
868	if (jstate && jstate->clocations_count > 0)
869		pgss_store(pstate->p_sourcetext,
870				   query->queryId,
871				   query->stmt_location,
872				   query->stmt_len,
873				   PGSS_INVALID,
874				   0,
875				   0,
876				   NULL,
877				   NULL,
878				   NULL,
879				   jstate,
880				   0,
881				   0);
882}
883
884/*
885 * Planner hook: forward to regular planner, but measure planning time
886 * if needed.
887 */ view japanese
888static PlannedStmt *
889pgss_planner(Query *parse,
890			 const char *query_string,
891			 int cursorOptions,
892			 ParamListInfo boundParams)
893{
894	PlannedStmt *result;
895
896	/*
897	 * We can't process the query if no query_string is provided, as
898	 * pgss_store needs it.  We also ignore query without queryid, as it would
899	 * be treated as a utility statement, which may not be the case.
900	 */ view japanese
901	if (pgss_enabled(nesting_level)
902		&& pgss_track_planning && query_string
903		&& parse->queryId != INT64CONST(0))
904	{
905		instr_time	start;
906		instr_time	duration;
907		BufferUsage bufusage_start,
908					bufusage;
909		WalUsage	walusage_start,
910					walusage;
911
912		/* We need to track buffer usage as the planner can access them. */ view japanese
913		bufusage_start = pgBufferUsage;
914
915		/*
916		 * Similarly the planner could write some WAL records in some cases
917		 * (e.g. setting a hint bit with those being WAL-logged)
918		 */ view japanese
919		walusage_start = pgWalUsage;
920		INSTR_TIME_SET_CURRENT(start);
921
922		nesting_level++;
923		PG_TRY();
924		{
925			if (prev_planner_hook)
926				result = prev_planner_hook(parse, query_string, cursorOptions,
927										   boundParams);
928			else
929				result = standard_planner(parse, query_string, cursorOptions,
930										  boundParams);
931		}
932		PG_FINALLY();
933		{
934			nesting_level--;
935		}
936		PG_END_TRY();
937
938		INSTR_TIME_SET_CURRENT(duration);
939		INSTR_TIME_SUBTRACT(duration, start);
940
941		/* calc differences of buffer counters. */
942		memset(&bufusage, 0, sizeof(BufferUsage));
943		BufferUsageAccumDiff(&bufusage, &pgBufferUsage, &bufusage_start);
944
945		/* calc differences of WAL counters. */
946		memset(&walusage, 0, sizeof(WalUsage));
947		WalUsageAccumDiff(&walusage, &pgWalUsage, &walusage_start);
948
949		pgss_store(query_string,
950				   parse->queryId,
951				   parse->stmt_location,
952				   parse->stmt_len,
953				   PGSS_PLAN,
954				   INSTR_TIME_GET_MILLISEC(duration),
955				   0,
956				   &bufusage,
957				   &walusage,
958				   NULL,
959				   NULL,
960				   0,
961				   0);
962	}
963	else
964	{
965		/*
966		 * Even though we're not tracking plan time for this statement, we
967		 * must still increment the nesting level, to ensure that functions
968		 * evaluated during planning are not seen as top-level calls.
969		 */ view japanese
970		nesting_level++;
971		PG_TRY();
972		{
973			if (prev_planner_hook)
974				result = prev_planner_hook(parse, query_string, cursorOptions,
975										   boundParams);
976			else
977				result = standard_planner(parse, query_string, cursorOptions,
978										  boundParams);
979		}
980		PG_FINALLY();
981		{
982			nesting_level--;
983		}
984		PG_END_TRY();
985	}
986
987	return result;
988}
989
990/*
991 * ExecutorStart hook: start up tracking if needed
992 */ view japanese
993static void
994pgss_ExecutorStart(QueryDesc *queryDesc, int eflags)
995{
996	if (prev_ExecutorStart)
997		prev_ExecutorStart(queryDesc, eflags);
998	else
999		standard_ExecutorStart(queryDesc, eflags);
1000
1001	/*
1002	 * If query has queryId zero, don't track it.  This prevents double
1003	 * counting of optimizable statements that are directly contained in
1004	 * utility statements.
1005	 */ view japanese
1006	if (pgss_enabled(nesting_level) && queryDesc->plannedstmt->queryId != INT64CONST(0))
1007	{
1008		/*
1009		 * Set up to track total elapsed time in ExecutorRun.  Make sure the
1010		 * space is allocated in the per-query context so it will go away at
1011		 * ExecutorEnd.
1012		 */ view japanese
1013		if (queryDesc->totaltime == NULL)
1014		{
1015			MemoryContext oldcxt;
1016
1017			oldcxt = MemoryContextSwitchTo(queryDesc->estate->es_query_cxt);
1018			queryDesc->totaltime = InstrAlloc(1, INSTRUMENT_ALL, false);
1019			MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcxt);
1020		}
1021	}
1022}
1023
1024/*
1025 * ExecutorRun hook: all we need do is track nesting depth
1026 */ view japanese
1027static void
1028pgss_ExecutorRun(QueryDesc *queryDesc, ScanDirection direction, uint64 count)
1029{
1030	nesting_level++;
1031	PG_TRY();
1032	{
1033		if (prev_ExecutorRun)
1034			prev_ExecutorRun(queryDesc, direction, count);
1035		else
1036			standard_ExecutorRun(queryDesc, direction, count);
1037	}
1038	PG_FINALLY();
1039	{
1040		nesting_level--;
1041	}
1042	PG_END_TRY();
1043}
1044
1045/*
1046 * ExecutorFinish hook: all we need do is track nesting depth
1047 */ view japanese
1048static void
1049pgss_ExecutorFinish(QueryDesc *queryDesc)
1050{
1051	nesting_level++;
1052	PG_TRY();
1053	{
1054		if (prev_ExecutorFinish)
1055			prev_ExecutorFinish(queryDesc);
1056		else
1057			standard_ExecutorFinish(queryDesc);
1058	}
1059	PG_FINALLY();
1060	{
1061		nesting_level--;
1062	}
1063	PG_END_TRY();
1064}
1065
1066/*
1067 * ExecutorEnd hook: store results if needed
1068 */ view japanese
1069static void
1070pgss_ExecutorEnd(QueryDesc *queryDesc)
1071{
1072	int64		queryId = queryDesc->plannedstmt->queryId;
1073
1074	if (queryId != INT64CONST(0) && queryDesc->totaltime &&
1075		pgss_enabled(nesting_level))
1076	{
1077		/*
1078		 * Make sure stats accumulation is done.  (Note: it's okay if several
1079		 * levels of hook all do this.)
1080		 */ view japanese
1081		InstrEndLoop(queryDesc->totaltime);
1082
1083		pgss_store(queryDesc->sourceText,
1084				   queryId,
1085				   queryDesc->plannedstmt->stmt_location,
1086				   queryDesc->plannedstmt->stmt_len,
1087				   PGSS_EXEC,
1088				   queryDesc->totaltime->total * 1000.0,	/* convert to msec */ view japanese
1089				   queryDesc->estate->es_total_processed,
1090				   &queryDesc->totaltime->bufusage,
1091				   &queryDesc->totaltime->walusage,
1092				   queryDesc->estate->es_jit ? &queryDesc->estate->es_jit->instr : NULL,
1093				   NULL,
1094				   queryDesc->estate->es_parallel_workers_to_launch,
1095				   queryDesc->estate->es_parallel_workers_launched);
1096	}
1097
1098	if (prev_ExecutorEnd)
1099		prev_ExecutorEnd(queryDesc);
1100	else
1101		standard_ExecutorEnd(queryDesc);
1102}
1103
1104/*
1105 * ProcessUtility hook
1106 */ view japanese
1107static void
1108pgss_ProcessUtility(PlannedStmt *pstmt, const char *queryString,
1109					bool readOnlyTree,
1110					ProcessUtilityContext context,
1111					ParamListInfo params, QueryEnvironment *queryEnv,
1112					DestReceiver *dest, QueryCompletion *qc)
1113{
1114	Node	   *parsetree = pstmt->utilityStmt;
1115	int64		saved_queryId = pstmt->queryId;
1116	int			saved_stmt_location = pstmt->stmt_location;
1117	int			saved_stmt_len = pstmt->stmt_len;
1118	bool		enabled = pgss_track_utility && pgss_enabled(nesting_level);
1119
1120	/*
1121	 * Force utility statements to get queryId zero.  We do this even in cases
1122	 * where the statement contains an optimizable statement for which a
1123	 * queryId could be derived (such as EXPLAIN or DECLARE CURSOR).  For such
1124	 * cases, runtime control will first go through ProcessUtility and then
1125	 * the executor, and we don't want the executor hooks to do anything,
1126	 * since we are already measuring the statement's costs at the utility
1127	 * level.
1128	 *
1129	 * Note that this is only done if pg_stat_statements is enabled and
1130	 * configured to track utility statements, in the unlikely possibility
1131	 * that user configured another extension to handle utility statements
1132	 * only.
1133	 */ view japanese
1134	if (enabled)
1135		pstmt->queryId = INT64CONST(0);
1136
1137	/*
1138	 * If it's an EXECUTE statement, we don't track it and don't increment the
1139	 * nesting level.  This allows the cycles to be charged to the underlying
1140	 * PREPARE instead (by the Executor hooks), which is much more useful.
1141	 *
1142	 * We also don't track execution of PREPARE.  If we did, we would get one
1143	 * hash table entry for the PREPARE (with hash calculated from the query
1144	 * string), and then a different one with the same query string (but hash
1145	 * calculated from the query tree) would be used to accumulate costs of
1146	 * ensuing EXECUTEs.  This would be confusing.  Since PREPARE doesn't
1147	 * actually run the planner (only parse+rewrite), its costs are generally
1148	 * pretty negligible and it seems okay to just ignore it.
1149	 */ view japanese
1150	if (enabled &&
1151		!IsA(parsetree, ExecuteStmt) &&
1152		!IsA(parsetree, PrepareStmt))
1153	{
1154		instr_time	start;
1155		instr_time	duration;
1156		uint64		rows;
1157		BufferUsage bufusage_start,
1158					bufusage;
1159		WalUsage	walusage_start,
1160					walusage;
1161
1162		bufusage_start = pgBufferUsage;
1163		walusage_start = pgWalUsage;
1164		INSTR_TIME_SET_CURRENT(start);
1165
1166		nesting_level++;
1167		PG_TRY();
1168		{
1169			if (prev_ProcessUtility)
1170				prev_ProcessUtility(pstmt, queryString, readOnlyTree,
1171									context, params, queryEnv,
1172									dest, qc);
1173			else
1174				standard_ProcessUtility(pstmt, queryString, readOnlyTree,
1175										context, params, queryEnv,
1176										dest, qc);
1177		}
1178		PG_FINALLY();
1179		{
1180			nesting_level--;
1181		}
1182		PG_END_TRY();
1183
1184		/*
1185		 * CAUTION: do not access the *pstmt data structure again below here.
1186		 * If it was a ROLLBACK or similar, that data structure may have been
1187		 * freed.  We must copy everything we still need into local variables,
1188		 * which we did above.
1189		 *
1190		 * For the same reason, we can't risk restoring pstmt->queryId to its
1191		 * former value, which'd otherwise be a good idea.
1192		 */ view japanese
1193
1194		INSTR_TIME_SET_CURRENT(duration);
1195		INSTR_TIME_SUBTRACT(duration, start);
1196
1197		/*
1198		 * Track the total number of rows retrieved or affected by the utility
1199		 * statements of COPY, FETCH, CREATE TABLE AS, CREATE MATERIALIZED
1200		 * VIEW, REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW and SELECT INTO.
1201		 */ view japanese
1202		rows = (qc && (qc->commandTag == CMDTAG_COPY ||
1203					   qc->commandTag == CMDTAG_FETCH ||
1204					   qc->commandTag == CMDTAG_SELECT ||
1205					   qc->commandTag == CMDTAG_REFRESH_MATERIALIZED_VIEW)) ?
1206			qc->nprocessed : 0;
1207
1208		/* calc differences of buffer counters. */ view japanese
1209		memset(&bufusage, 0, sizeof(BufferUsage));
1210		BufferUsageAccumDiff(&bufusage, &pgBufferUsage, &bufusage_start);
1211
1212		/* calc differences of WAL counters. */ view japanese
1213		memset(&walusage, 0, sizeof(WalUsage));
1214		WalUsageAccumDiff(&walusage, &pgWalUsage, &walusage_start);
1215
1216		pgss_store(queryString,
1217				   saved_queryId,
1218				   saved_stmt_location,
1219				   saved_stmt_len,
1220				   PGSS_EXEC,
1221				   INSTR_TIME_GET_MILLISEC(duration),
1222				   rows,
1223				   &bufusage,
1224				   &walusage,
1225				   NULL,
1226				   NULL,
1227				   0,
1228				   0);
1229	}
1230	else
1231	{
1232		/*
1233		 * Even though we're not tracking execution time for this statement,
1234		 * we must still increment the nesting level, to ensure that functions
1235		 * evaluated within it are not seen as top-level calls.  But don't do
1236		 * so for EXECUTE; that way, when control reaches pgss_planner or
1237		 * pgss_ExecutorStart, we will treat the costs as top-level if
1238		 * appropriate.  Likewise, don't bump for PREPARE, so that parse
1239		 * analysis will treat the statement as top-level if appropriate.
1240		 *
1241		 * To be absolutely certain we don't mess up the nesting level,
1242		 * evaluate the bump_level condition just once.
1243		 */ view japanese
1244		bool		bump_level =
1245			!IsA(parsetree, ExecuteStmt) &&
1246			!IsA(parsetree, PrepareStmt);
1247
1248		if (bump_level)
1249			nesting_level++;
1250		PG_TRY();
1251		{
1252			if (prev_ProcessUtility)
1253				prev_ProcessUtility(pstmt, queryString, readOnlyTree,
1254									context, params, queryEnv,
1255									dest, qc);
1256			else
1257				standard_ProcessUtility(pstmt, queryString, readOnlyTree,
1258										context, params, queryEnv,
1259										dest, qc);
1260		}
1261		PG_FINALLY();
1262		{
1263			if (bump_level)
1264				nesting_level--;
1265		}
1266		PG_END_TRY();
1267	}
1268}
1269
1270/*
1271 * Store some statistics for a statement.
1272 *
1273 * If jstate is not NULL then we're trying to create an entry for which
1274 * we have no statistics as yet; we just want to record the normalized
1275 * query string.  total_time, rows, bufusage and walusage are ignored in this
1276 * case.
1277 *
1278 * If kind is PGSS_PLAN or PGSS_EXEC, its value is used as the array position
1279 * for the arrays in the Counters field.
1280 */ view japanese
1281static void
1282pgss_store(const char *query, int64 queryId,
1283		   int query_location, int query_len,
1284		   pgssStoreKind kind,
1285		   double total_time, uint64 rows,
1286		   const BufferUsage *bufusage,
1287		   const WalUsage *walusage,
1288		   const struct JitInstrumentation *jitusage,
1289		   JumbleState *jstate,
1290		   int parallel_workers_to_launch,
1291		   int parallel_workers_launched)
1292{
1293	pgssHashKey key;
1294	pgssEntry  *entry;
1295	char	   *norm_query = NULL;
1296	int			encoding = GetDatabaseEncoding();
1297
1298	Assert(query != NULL);
1299
1300	/* Safety check... */ view japanese
1301	if (!pgss || !pgss_hash)
1302		return;
1303
1304	/*
1305	 * Nothing to do if compute_query_id isn't enabled and no other module
1306	 * computed a query identifier.
1307	 */ view japanese
1308	if (queryId == INT64CONST(0))
1309		return;
1310
1311	/*
1312	 * Confine our attention to the relevant part of the string, if the query
1313	 * is a portion of a multi-statement source string, and update query
1314	 * location and length if needed.
1315	 */ view japanese
1316	query = CleanQuerytext(query, &query_location, &query_len);
1317
1318	/* Set up key for hashtable search */ view japanese
1319
1320	/* clear padding */ view japanese
1321	memset(&key, 0, sizeof(pgssHashKey));
1322
1323	key.userid = GetUserId();
1324	key.dbid = MyDatabaseId;
1325	key.queryid = queryId;
1326	key.toplevel = (nesting_level == 0);
1327
1328	/* Lookup the hash table entry with shared lock. */ view japanese
1329	LWLockAcquire(pgss->lock, LW_SHARED);
1330
1331	entry = (pgssEntry *) hash_search(pgss_hash, &key, HASH_FIND, NULL);
1332
1333	/* Create new entry, if not present */ view japanese
1334	if (!entry)
1335	{
1336		Size		query_offset;
1337		int			gc_count;
1338		bool		stored;
1339		bool		do_gc;
1340
1341		/*
1342		 * Create a new, normalized query string if caller asked.  We don't
1343		 * need to hold the lock while doing this work.  (Note: in any case,
1344		 * it's possible that someone else creates a duplicate hashtable entry
1345		 * in the interval where we don't hold the lock below.  That case is
1346		 * handled by entry_alloc.)
1347		 */ view japanese
1348		if (jstate)
1349		{
1350			LWLockRelease(pgss->lock);
1351			norm_query = generate_normalized_query(jstate, query,
1352												   query_location,
1353												   &query_len);
1354			LWLockAcquire(pgss->lock, LW_SHARED);
1355		}
1356
1357		/* Append new query text to file with only shared lock held */ view japanese
1358		stored = qtext_store(norm_query ? norm_query : query, query_len,
1359							 &query_offset, &gc_count);
1360
1361		/*
1362		 * Determine whether we need to garbage collect external query texts
1363		 * while the shared lock is still held.  This micro-optimization
1364		 * avoids taking the time to decide this while holding exclusive lock.
1365		 */ view japanese
1366		do_gc = need_gc_qtexts();
1367
1368		/* Need exclusive lock to make a new hashtable entry - promote */ view japanese
1369		LWLockRelease(pgss->lock);
1370		LWLockAcquire(pgss->lock, LW_EXCLUSIVE);
1371
1372		/*
1373		 * A garbage collection may have occurred while we weren't holding the
1374		 * lock.  In the unlikely event that this happens, the query text we
1375		 * stored above will have been garbage collected, so write it again.
1376		 * This should be infrequent enough that doing it while holding
1377		 * exclusive lock isn't a performance problem.
1378		 */ view japanese
1379		if (!stored || pgss->gc_count != gc_count)
1380			stored = qtext_store(norm_query ? norm_query : query, query_len,
1381								 &query_offset, NULL);
1382
1383		/* If we failed to write to the text file, give up */ view japanese
1384		if (!stored)
1385			goto done;
1386
1387		/* OK to create a new hashtable entry */ view japanese
1388		entry = entry_alloc(&key, query_offset, query_len, encoding,
1389							jstate != NULL);
1390
1391		/* If needed, perform garbage collection while exclusive lock held */ view japanese
1392		if (do_gc)
1393			gc_qtexts();
1394	}
1395
1396	/* Increment the counts, except when jstate is not NULL */ view japanese
1397	if (!jstate)
1398	{
1399		Assert(kind == PGSS_PLAN || kind == PGSS_EXEC);
1400
1401		/*
1402		 * Grab the spinlock while updating the counters (see comment about
1403		 * locking rules at the head of the file)
1404		 */ view japanese
1405		SpinLockAcquire(&entry->mutex);
1406
1407		/* "Unstick" entry if it was previously sticky */ view japanese
1408		if (IS_STICKY(entry->counters))
1409			entry->counters.usage = USAGE_INIT;
1410
1411		entry->counters.calls[kind] += 1;
1412		entry->counters.total_time[kind] += total_time;
1413
1414		if (entry->counters.calls[kind] == 1)
1415		{
1416			entry->counters.min_time[kind] = total_time;
1417			entry->counters.max_time[kind] = total_time;
1418			entry->counters.mean_time[kind] = total_time;
1419		}
1420		else
1421		{
1422			/*
1423			 * Welford's method for accurately computing variance. See
1424			 * <http://www.johndcook.com/blog/standard_deviation/>
1425			 */ view japanese
1426			double		old_mean = entry->counters.mean_time[kind];
1427
1428			entry->counters.mean_time[kind] +=
1429				(total_time - old_mean) / entry->counters.calls[kind];
1430			entry->counters.sum_var_time[kind] +=
1431				(total_time - old_mean) * (total_time - entry->counters.mean_time[kind]);
1432
1433			/*
1434			 * Calculate min and max time. min = 0 and max = 0 means that the
1435			 * min/max statistics were reset
1436			 */ view japanese
1437			if (entry->counters.min_time[kind] == 0
1438				&& entry->counters.max_time[kind] == 0)
1439			{
1440				entry->counters.min_time[kind] = total_time;
1441				entry->counters.max_time[kind] = total_time;
1442			}
1443			else
1444			{
1445				if (entry->counters.min_time[kind] > total_time)
1446					entry->counters.min_time[kind] = total_time;
1447				if (entry->counters.max_time[kind] < total_time)
1448					entry->counters.max_time[kind] = total_time;
1449			}
1450		}
1451		entry->counters.rows += rows;
1452		entry->counters.shared_blks_hit += bufusage->shared_blks_hit;
1453		entry->counters.shared_blks_read += bufusage->shared_blks_read;
1454		entry->counters.shared_blks_dirtied += bufusage->shared_blks_dirtied;
1455		entry->counters.shared_blks_written += bufusage->shared_blks_written;
1456		entry->counters.local_blks_hit += bufusage->local_blks_hit;
1457		entry->counters.local_blks_read += bufusage->local_blks_read;
1458		entry->counters.local_blks_dirtied += bufusage->local_blks_dirtied;
1459		entry->counters.local_blks_written += bufusage->local_blks_written;
1460		entry->counters.temp_blks_read += bufusage->temp_blks_read;
1461		entry->counters.temp_blks_written += bufusage->temp_blks_written;
1462		entry->counters.shared_blk_read_time += INSTR_TIME_GET_MILLISEC(bufusage->shared_blk_read_time);
1463		entry->counters.shared_blk_write_time += INSTR_TIME_GET_MILLISEC(bufusage->shared_blk_write_time);
1464		entry->counters.local_blk_read_time += INSTR_TIME_GET_MILLISEC(bufusage->local_blk_read_time);
1465		entry->counters.local_blk_write_time += INSTR_TIME_GET_MILLISEC(bufusage->local_blk_write_time);
1466		entry->counters.temp_blk_read_time += INSTR_TIME_GET_MILLISEC(bufusage->temp_blk_read_time);
1467		entry->counters.temp_blk_write_time += INSTR_TIME_GET_MILLISEC(bufusage->temp_blk_write_time);
1468		entry->counters.usage += USAGE_EXEC(total_time);
1469		entry->counters.wal_records += walusage->wal_records;
1470		entry->counters.wal_fpi += walusage->wal_fpi;
1471		entry->counters.wal_bytes += walusage->wal_bytes;
1472		entry->counters.wal_buffers_full += walusage->wal_buffers_full;
1473		if (jitusage)
1474		{
1475			entry->counters.jit_functions += jitusage->created_functions;
1476			entry->counters.jit_generation_time += INSTR_TIME_GET_MILLISEC(jitusage->generation_counter);
1477
1478			if (INSTR_TIME_GET_MILLISEC(jitusage->deform_counter))
1479				entry->counters.jit_deform_count++;
1480			entry->counters.jit_deform_time += INSTR_TIME_GET_MILLISEC(jitusage->deform_counter);
1481
1482			if (INSTR_TIME_GET_MILLISEC(jitusage->inlining_counter))
1483				entry->counters.jit_inlining_count++;
1484			entry->counters.jit_inlining_time += INSTR_TIME_GET_MILLISEC(jitusage->inlining_counter);
1485
1486			if (INSTR_TIME_GET_MILLISEC(jitusage->optimization_counter))
1487				entry->counters.jit_optimization_count++;
1488			entry->counters.jit_optimization_time += INSTR_TIME_GET_MILLISEC(jitusage->optimization_counter);
1489
1490			if (INSTR_TIME_GET_MILLISEC(jitusage->emission_counter))
1491				entry->counters.jit_emission_count++;
1492			entry->counters.jit_emission_time += INSTR_TIME_GET_MILLISEC(jitusage->emission_counter);
1493		}
1494
1495		/* parallel worker counters */ view japanese
1496		entry->counters.parallel_workers_to_launch += parallel_workers_to_launch;
1497		entry->counters.parallel_workers_launched += parallel_workers_launched;
1498
1499		SpinLockRelease(&entry->mutex);
1500	}
1501
1502done:
1503	LWLockRelease(pgss->lock);
1504
1505	/* We postpone this clean-up until we're out of the lock */ view japanese
1506	if (norm_query)
1507		pfree(norm_query);
1508}
1509
1510/*
1511 * Reset statement statistics corresponding to userid, dbid, and queryid.
1512 */ view japanese
1513Datum
1514pg_stat_statements_reset_1_7(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
1515{
1516	Oid			userid;
1517	Oid			dbid;
1518	int64		queryid;
1519
1520	userid = PG_GETARG_OID(0);
1521	dbid = PG_GETARG_OID(1);
1522	queryid = PG_GETARG_INT64(2);
1523
1524	entry_reset(userid, dbid, queryid, false);
1525
1526	PG_RETURN_VOID();
1527}
1528
1529Datum
1530pg_stat_statements_reset_1_11(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
1531{
1532	Oid			userid;
1533	Oid			dbid;
1534	int64		queryid;
1535	bool		minmax_only;
1536
1537	userid = PG_GETARG_OID(0);
1538	dbid = PG_GETARG_OID(1);
1539	queryid = PG_GETARG_INT64(2);
1540	minmax_only = PG_GETARG_BOOL(3);
1541
1542	PG_RETURN_TIMESTAMPTZ(entry_reset(userid, dbid, queryid, minmax_only));
1543}
1544
1545/*
1546 * Reset statement statistics.
1547 */ view japanese
1548Datum
1549pg_stat_statements_reset(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
1550{
1551	entry_reset(0, 0, 0, false);
1552
1553	PG_RETURN_VOID();
1554}
1555
1556/* Number of output arguments (columns) for various API versions */ view japanese
1557#define PG_STAT_STATEMENTS_COLS_V1_0	14
1558#define PG_STAT_STATEMENTS_COLS_V1_1	18
1559#define PG_STAT_STATEMENTS_COLS_V1_2	19
1560#define PG_STAT_STATEMENTS_COLS_V1_3	23
1561#define PG_STAT_STATEMENTS_COLS_V1_8	32
1562#define PG_STAT_STATEMENTS_COLS_V1_9	33
1563#define PG_STAT_STATEMENTS_COLS_V1_10	43
1564#define PG_STAT_STATEMENTS_COLS_V1_11	49
1565#define PG_STAT_STATEMENTS_COLS_V1_12	52
1566#define PG_STAT_STATEMENTS_COLS			52	/* maximum of above */ view japanese
1567
1568/*
1569 * Retrieve statement statistics.
1570 *
1571 * The SQL API of this function has changed multiple times, and will likely
1572 * do so again in future.  To support the case where a newer version of this
1573 * loadable module is being used with an old SQL declaration of the function,
1574 * we continue to support the older API versions.  For 1.2 and later, the
1575 * expected API version is identified by embedding it in the C name of the
1576 * function.  Unfortunately we weren't bright enough to do that for 1.1.
1577 */ view japanese
1578Datum
1579pg_stat_statements_1_12(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
1580{
1581	bool		showtext = PG_GETARG_BOOL(0);
1582
1583	pg_stat_statements_internal(fcinfo, PGSS_V1_12, showtext);
1584
1585	return (Datum) 0;
1586}
1587
1588Datum
1589pg_stat_statements_1_11(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
1590{
1591	bool		showtext = PG_GETARG_BOOL(0);
1592
1593	pg_stat_statements_internal(fcinfo, PGSS_V1_11, showtext);
1594
1595	return (Datum) 0;
1596}
1597
1598Datum
1599pg_stat_statements_1_10(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
1600{
1601	bool		showtext = PG_GETARG_BOOL(0);
1602
1603	pg_stat_statements_internal(fcinfo, PGSS_V1_10, showtext);
1604
1605	return (Datum) 0;
1606}
1607
1608Datum
1609pg_stat_statements_1_9(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
1610{
1611	bool		showtext = PG_GETARG_BOOL(0);
1612
1613	pg_stat_statements_internal(fcinfo, PGSS_V1_9, showtext);
1614
1615	return (Datum) 0;
1616}
1617
1618Datum
1619pg_stat_statements_1_8(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
1620{
1621	bool		showtext = PG_GETARG_BOOL(0);
1622
1623	pg_stat_statements_internal(fcinfo, PGSS_V1_8, showtext);
1624
1625	return (Datum) 0;
1626}
1627
1628Datum
1629pg_stat_statements_1_3(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
1630{
1631	bool		showtext = PG_GETARG_BOOL(0);
1632
1633	pg_stat_statements_internal(fcinfo, PGSS_V1_3, showtext);
1634
1635	return (Datum) 0;
1636}
1637
1638Datum
1639pg_stat_statements_1_2(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
1640{
1641	bool		showtext = PG_GETARG_BOOL(0);
1642
1643	pg_stat_statements_internal(fcinfo, PGSS_V1_2, showtext);
1644
1645	return (Datum) 0;
1646}
1647
1648/*
1649 * Legacy entry point for pg_stat_statements() API versions 1.0 and 1.1.
1650 * This can be removed someday, perhaps.
1651 */ view japanese
1652Datum
1653pg_stat_statements(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
1654{
1655	/* If it's really API 1.1, we'll figure that out below */ view japanese
1656	pg_stat_statements_internal(fcinfo, PGSS_V1_0, true);
1657
1658	return (Datum) 0;
1659}
1660
1661/* Common code for all versions of pg_stat_statements() */ view japanese
1662static void
1663pg_stat_statements_internal(FunctionCallInfo fcinfo,
1664							pgssVersion api_version,
1665							bool showtext)
1666{
1667	ReturnSetInfo *rsinfo = (ReturnSetInfo *) fcinfo->resultinfo;
1668	Oid			userid = GetUserId();
1669	bool		is_allowed_role = false;
1670	char	   *qbuffer = NULL;
1671	Size		qbuffer_size = 0;
1672	Size		extent = 0;
1673	int			gc_count = 0;
1674	HASH_SEQ_STATUS hash_seq;
1675	pgssEntry  *entry;
1676
1677	/*
1678	 * Superusers or roles with the privileges of pg_read_all_stats members
1679	 * are allowed
1680	 */ view japanese
1681	is_allowed_role = has_privs_of_role(userid, ROLE_PG_READ_ALL_STATS);
1682
1683	/* hash table must exist already */ view japanese
1684	if (!pgss || !pgss_hash)
1685		ereport(ERROR,
1686				(errcode(ERRCODE_OBJECT_NOT_IN_PREREQUISITE_STATE),
1687				 errmsg("pg_stat_statements must be loaded via \"shared_preload_libraries\"")));
1688
1689	InitMaterializedSRF(fcinfo, 0);
1690
1691	/*
1692	 * Check we have the expected number of output arguments.  Aside from
1693	 * being a good safety check, we need a kluge here to detect API version
1694	 * 1.1, which was wedged into the code in an ill-considered way.
1695	 */ view japanese
1696	switch (rsinfo->setDesc->natts)
1697	{
1698		case PG_STAT_STATEMENTS_COLS_V1_0:
1699			if (api_version != PGSS_V1_0)
1700				elog(ERROR, "incorrect number of output arguments");
1701			break;
1702		case PG_STAT_STATEMENTS_COLS_V1_1:
1703			/* pg_stat_statements() should have told us 1.0 */ view japanese
1704			if (api_version != PGSS_V1_0)
1705				elog(ERROR, "incorrect number of output arguments");
1706			api_version = PGSS_V1_1;
1707			break;
1708		case PG_STAT_STATEMENTS_COLS_V1_2:
1709			if (api_version != PGSS_V1_2)
1710				elog(ERROR, "incorrect number of output arguments");
1711			break;
1712		case PG_STAT_STATEMENTS_COLS_V1_3:
1713			if (api_version != PGSS_V1_3)
1714				elog(ERROR, "incorrect number of output arguments");
1715			break;
1716		case PG_STAT_STATEMENTS_COLS_V1_8:
1717			if (api_version != PGSS_V1_8)
1718				elog(ERROR, "incorrect number of output arguments");
1719			break;
1720		case PG_STAT_STATEMENTS_COLS_V1_9:
1721			if (api_version != PGSS_V1_9)
1722				elog(ERROR, "incorrect number of output arguments");
1723			break;
1724		case PG_STAT_STATEMENTS_COLS_V1_10:
1725			if (api_version != PGSS_V1_10)
1726				elog(ERROR, "incorrect number of output arguments");
1727			break;
1728		case PG_STAT_STATEMENTS_COLS_V1_11:
1729			if (api_version != PGSS_V1_11)
1730				elog(ERROR, "incorrect number of output arguments");
1731			break;
1732		case PG_STAT_STATEMENTS_COLS_V1_12:
1733			if (api_version != PGSS_V1_12)
1734				elog(ERROR, "incorrect number of output arguments");
1735			break;
1736		default:
1737			elog(ERROR, "incorrect number of output arguments");
1738	}
1739
1740	/*
1741	 * We'd like to load the query text file (if needed) while not holding any
1742	 * lock on pgss->lock.  In the worst case we'll have to do this again
1743	 * after we have the lock, but it's unlikely enough to make this a win
1744	 * despite occasional duplicated work.  We need to reload if anybody
1745	 * writes to the file (either a retail qtext_store(), or a garbage
1746	 * collection) between this point and where we've gotten shared lock.  If
1747	 * a qtext_store is actually in progress when we look, we might as well
1748	 * skip the speculative load entirely.
1749	 */ view japanese
1750	if (showtext)
1751	{
1752		int			n_writers;
1753
1754		/* Take the mutex so we can examine variables */ view japanese
1755		SpinLockAcquire(&pgss->mutex);
1756		extent = pgss->extent;
1757		n_writers = pgss->n_writers;
1758		gc_count = pgss->gc_count;
1759		SpinLockRelease(&pgss->mutex);
1760
1761		/* No point in loading file now if there are active writers */ view japanese
1762		if (n_writers == 0)
1763			qbuffer = qtext_load_file(&qbuffer_size);
1764	}
1765
1766	/*
1767	 * Get shared lock, load or reload the query text file if we must, and
1768	 * iterate over the hashtable entries.
1769	 *
1770	 * With a large hash table, we might be holding the lock rather longer
1771	 * than one could wish.  However, this only blocks creation of new hash
1772	 * table entries, and the larger the hash table the less likely that is to
1773	 * be needed.  So we can hope this is okay.  Perhaps someday we'll decide
1774	 * we need to partition the hash table to limit the time spent holding any
1775	 * one lock.
1776	 */ view japanese
1777	LWLockAcquire(pgss->lock, LW_SHARED);
1778
1779	if (showtext)
1780	{
1781		/*
1782		 * Here it is safe to examine extent and gc_count without taking the
1783		 * mutex.  Note that although other processes might change
1784		 * pgss->extent just after we look at it, the strings they then write
1785		 * into the file cannot yet be referenced in the hashtable, so we
1786		 * don't care whether we see them or not.
1787		 *
1788		 * If qtext_load_file fails, we just press on; we'll return NULL for
1789		 * every query text.
1790		 */ view japanese
1791		if (qbuffer == NULL ||
1792			pgss->extent != extent ||
1793			pgss->gc_count != gc_count)
1794		{
1795			if (qbuffer)
1796				pfree(qbuffer);
1797			qbuffer = qtext_load_file(&qbuffer_size);
1798		}
1799	}
1800
1801	hash_seq_init(&hash_seq, pgss_hash);
1802	while ((entry = hash_seq_search(&hash_seq)) != NULL)
1803	{
1804		Datum		values[PG_STAT_STATEMENTS_COLS];
1805		bool		nulls[PG_STAT_STATEMENTS_COLS];
1806		int			i = 0;
1807		Counters	tmp;
1808		double		stddev;
1809		int64		queryid = entry->key.queryid;
1810		TimestampTz stats_since;
1811		TimestampTz minmax_stats_since;
1812
1813		memset(values, 0, sizeof(values));
1814		memset(nulls, 0, sizeof(nulls));
1815
1816		values[i++] = ObjectIdGetDatum(entry->key.userid);
1817		values[i++] = ObjectIdGetDatum(entry->key.dbid);
1818		if (api_version >= PGSS_V1_9)
1819			values[i++] = BoolGetDatum(entry->key.toplevel);
1820
1821		if (is_allowed_role || entry->key.userid == userid)
1822		{
1823			if (api_version >= PGSS_V1_2)
1824				values[i++] = Int64GetDatumFast(queryid);
1825
1826			if (showtext)
1827			{
1828				char	   *qstr = qtext_fetch(entry->query_offset,
1829											   entry->query_len,
1830											   qbuffer,
1831											   qbuffer_size);
1832
1833				if (qstr)
1834				{
1835					char	   *enc;
1836
1837					enc = pg_any_to_server(qstr,
1838										   entry->query_len,
1839										   entry->encoding);
1840
1841					values[i++] = CStringGetTextDatum(enc);
1842
1843					if (enc != qstr)
1844						pfree(enc);
1845				}
1846				else
1847				{
1848					/* Just return a null if we fail to find the text */ view japanese
1849					nulls[i++] = true;
1850				}
1851			}
1852			else
1853			{
1854				/* Query text not requested */ view japanese
1855				nulls[i++] = true;
1856			}
1857		}
1858		else
1859		{
1860			/* Don't show queryid */ view japanese
1861			if (api_version >= PGSS_V1_2)
1862				nulls[i++] = true;
1863
1864			/*
1865			 * Don't show query text, but hint as to the reason for not doing
1866			 * so if it was requested
1867			 */ view japanese
1868			if (showtext)
1869				values[i++] = CStringGetTextDatum("<insufficient privilege>");
1870			else
1871				nulls[i++] = true;
1872		}
1873
1874		/* copy counters to a local variable to keep locking time short */ view japanese
1875		SpinLockAcquire(&entry->mutex);
1876		tmp = entry->counters;
1877		SpinLockRelease(&entry->mutex);
1878
1879		/*
1880		 * The spinlock is not required when reading these two as they are
1881		 * always updated when holding pgss->lock exclusively.
1882		 */ view japanese
1883		stats_since = entry->stats_since;
1884		minmax_stats_since = entry->minmax_stats_since;
1885
1886		/* Skip entry if unexecuted (ie, it's a pending "sticky" entry) */ view japanese
1887		if (IS_STICKY(tmp))
1888			continue;
1889
1890		/* Note that we rely on PGSS_PLAN being 0 and PGSS_EXEC being 1. */ view japanese
1891		for (int kind = 0; kind < PGSS_NUMKIND; kind++)
1892		{
1893			if (kind == PGSS_EXEC || api_version >= PGSS_V1_8)
1894			{
1895				values[i++] = Int64GetDatumFast(tmp.calls[kind]);
1896				values[i++] = Float8GetDatumFast(tmp.total_time[kind]);
1897			}
1898
1899			if ((kind == PGSS_EXEC && api_version >= PGSS_V1_3) ||
1900				api_version >= PGSS_V1_8)
1901			{
1902				values[i++] = Float8GetDatumFast(tmp.min_time[kind]);
1903				values[i++] = Float8GetDatumFast(tmp.max_time[kind]);
1904				values[i++] = Float8GetDatumFast(tmp.mean_time[kind]);
1905
1906				/*
1907				 * Note we are calculating the population variance here, not
1908				 * the sample variance, as we have data for the whole
1909				 * population, so Bessel's correction is not used, and we
1910				 * don't divide by tmp.calls - 1.
1911				 */ view japanese
1912				if (tmp.calls[kind] > 1)
1913					stddev = sqrt(tmp.sum_var_time[kind] / tmp.calls[kind]);
1914				else
1915					stddev = 0.0;
1916				values[i++] = Float8GetDatumFast(stddev);
1917			}
1918		}
1919		values[i++] = Int64GetDatumFast(tmp.rows);
1920		values[i++] = Int64GetDatumFast(tmp.shared_blks_hit);
1921		values[i++] = Int64GetDatumFast(tmp.shared_blks_read);
1922		if (api_version >= PGSS_V1_1)
1923			values[i++] = Int64GetDatumFast(tmp.shared_blks_dirtied);
1924		values[i++] = Int64GetDatumFast(tmp.shared_blks_written);
1925		values[i++] = Int64GetDatumFast(tmp.local_blks_hit);
1926		values[i++] = Int64GetDatumFast(tmp.local_blks_read);
1927		if (api_version >= PGSS_V1_1)
1928			values[i++] = Int64GetDatumFast(tmp.local_blks_dirtied);
1929		values[i++] = Int64GetDatumFast(tmp.local_blks_written);
1930		values[i++] = Int64GetDatumFast(tmp.temp_blks_read);
1931		values[i++] = Int64GetDatumFast(tmp.temp_blks_written);
1932		if (api_version >= PGSS_V1_1)
1933		{
1934			values[i++] = Float8GetDatumFast(tmp.shared_blk_read_time);
1935			values[i++] = Float8GetDatumFast(tmp.shared_blk_write_time);
1936		}
1937		if (api_version >= PGSS_V1_11)
1938		{
1939			values[i++] = Float8GetDatumFast(tmp.local_blk_read_time);
1940			values[i++] = Float8GetDatumFast(tmp.local_blk_write_time);
1941		}
1942		if (api_version >= PGSS_V1_10)
1943		{
1944			values[i++] = Float8GetDatumFast(tmp.temp_blk_read_time);
1945			values[i++] = Float8GetDatumFast(tmp.temp_blk_write_time);
1946		}
1947		if (api_version >= PGSS_V1_8)
1948		{
1949			char		buf[256];
1950			Datum		wal_bytes;
1951
1952			values[i++] = Int64GetDatumFast(tmp.wal_records);
1953			values[i++] = Int64GetDatumFast(tmp.wal_fpi);
1954
1955			snprintf(buf, sizeof buf, UINT64_FORMAT, tmp.wal_bytes);
1956
1957			/* Convert to numeric. */ view japanese
1958			wal_bytes = DirectFunctionCall3(numeric_in,
1959											CStringGetDatum(buf),
1960											ObjectIdGetDatum(0),
1961											Int32GetDatum(-1));
1962			values[i++] = wal_bytes;
1963		}
1964		if (api_version >= PGSS_V1_12)
1965		{
1966			values[i++] = Int64GetDatumFast(tmp.wal_buffers_full);
1967		}
1968		if (api_version >= PGSS_V1_10)
1969		{
1970			values[i++] = Int64GetDatumFast(tmp.jit_functions);
1971			values[i++] = Float8GetDatumFast(tmp.jit_generation_time);
1972			values[i++] = Int64GetDatumFast(tmp.jit_inlining_count);
1973			values[i++] = Float8GetDatumFast(tmp.jit_inlining_time);
1974			values[i++] = Int64GetDatumFast(tmp.jit_optimization_count);
1975			values[i++] = Float8GetDatumFast(tmp.jit_optimization_time);
1976			values[i++] = Int64GetDatumFast(tmp.jit_emission_count);
1977			values[i++] = Float8GetDatumFast(tmp.jit_emission_time);
1978		}
1979		if (api_version >= PGSS_V1_11)
1980		{
1981			values[i++] = Int64GetDatumFast(tmp.jit_deform_count);
1982			values[i++] = Float8GetDatumFast(tmp.jit_deform_time);
1983		}
1984		if (api_version >= PGSS_V1_12)
1985		{
1986			values[i++] = Int64GetDatumFast(tmp.parallel_workers_to_launch);
1987			values[i++] = Int64GetDatumFast(tmp.parallel_workers_launched);
1988		}
1989		if (api_version >= PGSS_V1_11)
1990		{
1991			values[i++] = TimestampTzGetDatum(stats_since);
1992			values[i++] = TimestampTzGetDatum(minmax_stats_since);
1993		}
1994
1995		Assert(i == (api_version == PGSS_V1_0 ? PG_STAT_STATEMENTS_COLS_V1_0 :
1996					 api_version == PGSS_V1_1 ? PG_STAT_STATEMENTS_COLS_V1_1 :
1997					 api_version == PGSS_V1_2 ? PG_STAT_STATEMENTS_COLS_V1_2 :
1998					 api_version == PGSS_V1_3 ? PG_STAT_STATEMENTS_COLS_V1_3 :
1999					 api_version == PGSS_V1_8 ? PG_STAT_STATEMENTS_COLS_V1_8 :
2000					 api_version == PGSS_V1_9 ? PG_STAT_STATEMENTS_COLS_V1_9 :
2001					 api_version == PGSS_V1_10 ? PG_STAT_STATEMENTS_COLS_V1_10 :
2002					 api_version == PGSS_V1_11 ? PG_STAT_STATEMENTS_COLS_V1_11 :
2003					 api_version == PGSS_V1_12 ? PG_STAT_STATEMENTS_COLS_V1_12 :
2004					 -1 /* fail if you forget to update this assert */ )); view japanese
2005
2006		tuplestore_putvalues(rsinfo->setResult, rsinfo->setDesc, values, nulls);
2007	}
2008
2009	LWLockRelease(pgss->lock);
2010
2011	if (qbuffer)
2012		pfree(qbuffer);
2013}
2014
2015/* Number of output arguments (columns) for pg_stat_statements_info */ view japanese
2016#define PG_STAT_STATEMENTS_INFO_COLS	2
2017
2018/*
2019 * Return statistics of pg_stat_statements.
2020 */ view japanese
2021Datum
2022pg_stat_statements_info(PG_FUNCTION_ARGS)
2023{
2024	pgssGlobalStats stats;
2025	TupleDesc	tupdesc;
2026	Datum		values[PG_STAT_STATEMENTS_INFO_COLS] = {0};
2027	bool		nulls[PG_STAT_STATEMENTS_INFO_COLS] = {0};
2028
2029	if (!pgss || !pgss_hash)
2030		ereport(ERROR,
2031				(errcode(ERRCODE_OBJECT_NOT_IN_PREREQUISITE_STATE),
2032				 errmsg("pg_stat_statements must be loaded via \"shared_preload_libraries\"")));
2033
2034	/* Build a tuple descriptor for our result type */ view japanese
2035	if (get_call_result_type(fcinfo, NULL, &tupdesc) != TYPEFUNC_COMPOSITE)
2036		elog(ERROR, "return type must be a row type");
2037
2038	/* Read global statistics for pg_stat_statements */ view japanese
2039	SpinLockAcquire(&pgss->mutex);
2040	stats = pgss->stats;
2041	SpinLockRelease(&pgss->mutex);
2042
2043	values[0] = Int64GetDatum(stats.dealloc);
2044	values[1] = TimestampTzGetDatum(stats.stats_reset);
2045
2046	PG_RETURN_DATUM(HeapTupleGetDatum(heap_form_tuple(tupdesc, values, nulls)));
2047}
2048
2049/*
2050 * Estimate shared memory space needed.
2051 */ view japanese
2052static Size
2053pgss_memsize(void)
2054{
2055	Size		size;
2056
2057	size = MAXALIGN(sizeof(pgssSharedState));
2058	size = add_size(size, hash_estimate_size(pgss_max, sizeof(pgssEntry)));
2059
2060	return size;
2061}
2062
2063/*
2064 * Allocate a new hashtable entry.
2065 * caller must hold an exclusive lock on pgss->lock
2066 *
2067 * "query" need not be null-terminated; we rely on query_len instead
2068 *
2069 * If "sticky" is true, make the new entry artificially sticky so that it will
2070 * probably still be there when the query finishes execution.  We do this by
2071 * giving it a median usage value rather than the normal value.  (Strictly
2072 * speaking, query strings are normalized on a best effort basis, though it
2073 * would be difficult to demonstrate this even under artificial conditions.)
2074 *
2075 * Note: despite needing exclusive lock, it's not an error for the target
2076 * entry to already exist.  This is because pgss_store releases and
2077 * reacquires lock after failing to find a match; so someone else could
2078 * have made the entry while we waited to get exclusive lock.
2079 */ view japanese
2080static pgssEntry *
2081entry_alloc(pgssHashKey *key, Size query_offset, int query_len, int encoding,
2082			bool sticky)
2083{
2084	pgssEntry  *entry;
2085	bool		found;
2086
2087	/* Make space if needed */ view japanese
2088	while (hash_get_num_entries(pgss_hash) >= pgss_max)
2089		entry_dealloc();
2090
2091	/* Find or create an entry with desired hash code */ view japanese
2092	entry = (pgssEntry *) hash_search(pgss_hash, key, HASH_ENTER, &found);
2093
2094	if (!found)
2095	{
2096		/* New entry, initialize it */ view japanese
2097
2098		/* reset the statistics */ view japanese
2099		memset(&entry->counters, 0, sizeof(Counters));
2100		/* set the appropriate initial usage count */ view japanese
2101		entry->counters.usage = sticky ? pgss->cur_median_usage : USAGE_INIT;
2102		/* re-initialize the mutex each time ... we assume no one using it */ view japanese
2103		SpinLockInit(&entry->mutex);
2104		/* ... and don't forget the query text metadata */ view japanese
2105		Assert(query_len >= 0);
2106		entry->query_offset = query_offset;
2107		entry->query_len = query_len;
2108		entry->encoding = encoding;
2109		entry->stats_since = GetCurrentTimestamp();
2110		entry->minmax_stats_since = entry->stats_since;
2111	}
2112
2113	return entry;
2114}
2115
2116/*
2117 * qsort comparator for sorting into increasing usage order
2118 */ view japanese
2119static int
2120entry_cmp(const void *lhs, const void *rhs)
2121{
2122	double		l_usage = (*(pgssEntry *const *) lhs)->counters.usage;
2123	double		r_usage = (*(pgssEntry *const *) rhs)->counters.usage;
2124
2125	if (l_usage < r_usage)
2126		return -1;
2127	else if (l_usage > r_usage)
2128		return +1;
2129	else
2130		return 0;
2131}
2132
2133/*
2134 * Deallocate least-used entries.
2135 *
2136 * Caller must hold an exclusive lock on pgss->lock.
2137 */ view japanese
2138static void
2139entry_dealloc(void)
2140{
2141	HASH_SEQ_STATUS hash_seq;
2142	pgssEntry **entries;
2143	pgssEntry  *entry;
2144	int			nvictims;
2145	int			i;
2146	Size		tottextlen;
2147	int			nvalidtexts;
2148
2149	/*
2150	 * Sort entries by usage and deallocate USAGE_DEALLOC_PERCENT of them.
2151	 * While we're scanning the table, apply the decay factor to the usage
2152	 * values, and update the mean query length.
2153	 *
2154	 * Note that the mean query length is almost immediately obsolete, since
2155	 * we compute it before not after discarding the least-used entries.
2156	 * Hopefully, that doesn't affect the mean too much; it doesn't seem worth
2157	 * making two passes to get a more current result.  Likewise, the new
2158	 * cur_median_usage includes the entries we're about to zap.
2159	 */ view japanese
2160
2161	entries = palloc(hash_get_num_entries(pgss_hash) * sizeof(pgssEntry *));
2162
2163	i = 0;
2164	tottextlen = 0;
2165	nvalidtexts = 0;
2166
2167	hash_seq_init(&hash_seq, pgss_hash);
2168	while ((entry = hash_seq_search(&hash_seq)) != NULL)
2169	{
2170		entries[i++] = entry;
2171		/* "Sticky" entries get a different usage decay rate. */ view japanese
2172		if (IS_STICKY(entry->counters))
2173			entry->counters.usage *= STICKY_DECREASE_FACTOR;
2174		else
2175			entry->counters.usage *= USAGE_DECREASE_FACTOR;
2176		/* In the mean length computation, ignore dropped texts. */ view japanese
2177		if (entry->query_len >= 0)
2178		{
2179			tottextlen += entry->query_len + 1;
2180			nvalidtexts++;
2181		}
2182	}
2183
2184	/* Sort into increasing order by usage */ view japanese
2185	qsort(entries, i, sizeof(pgssEntry *), entry_cmp);
2186
2187	/* Record the (approximate) median usage */ view japanese
2188	if (i > 0)
2189		pgss->cur_median_usage = entries[i / 2]->counters.usage;
2190	/* Record the mean query length */ view japanese
2191	if (nvalidtexts > 0)
2192		pgss->mean_query_len = tottextlen / nvalidtexts;
2193	else
2194		pgss->mean_query_len = ASSUMED_LENGTH_INIT;
2195
2196	/* Now zap an appropriate fraction of lowest-usage entries */ view japanese
2197	nvictims = Max(10, i * USAGE_DEALLOC_PERCENT / 100);
2198	nvictims = Min(nvictims, i);
2199
2200	for (i = 0; i < nvictims; i++)
2201	{
2202		hash_search(pgss_hash, &entries[i]->key, HASH_REMOVE, NULL);
2203	}
2204
2205	pfree(entries);
2206
2207	/* Increment the number of times entries are deallocated */ view japanese
2208	SpinLockAcquire(&pgss->mutex);
2209	pgss->stats.dealloc += 1;
2210	SpinLockRelease(&pgss->mutex);
2211}
2212
2213/*
2214 * Given a query string (not necessarily null-terminated), allocate a new
2215 * entry in the external query text file and store the string there.
2216 *
2217 * If successful, returns true, and stores the new entry's offset in the file
2218 * into *query_offset.  Also, if gc_count isn't NULL, *gc_count is set to the
2219 * number of garbage collections that have occurred so far.
2220 *
2221 * On failure, returns false.
2222 *
2223 * At least a shared lock on pgss->lock must be held by the caller, so as
2224 * to prevent a concurrent garbage collection.  Share-lock-holding callers
2225 * should pass a gc_count pointer to obtain the number of garbage collections,
2226 * so that they can recheck the count after obtaining exclusive lock to
2227 * detect whether a garbage collection occurred (and removed this entry).
2228 */ view japanese
2229static bool
2230qtext_store(const char *query, int query_len,
2231			Size *query_offset, int *gc_count)
2232{
2233	Size		off;
2234	int			fd;
2235
2236	/*
2237	 * We use a spinlock to protect extent/n_writers/gc_count, so that
2238	 * multiple processes may execute this function concurrently.
2239	 */ view japanese
2240	SpinLockAcquire(&pgss->mutex);
2241	off = pgss->extent;
2242	pgss->extent += query_len + 1;
2243	pgss->n_writers++;
2244	if (gc_count)
2245		*gc_count = pgss->gc_count;
2246	SpinLockRelease(&pgss->mutex);
2247
2248	*query_offset = off;
2249
2250	/*
2251	 * Don't allow the file to grow larger than what qtext_load_file can
2252	 * (theoretically) handle.  This has been seen to be reachable on 32-bit
2253	 * platforms.
2254	 */ view japanese
2255	if (unlikely(query_len >= MaxAllocHugeSize - off))
2256	{
2257		errno = EFBIG;			/* not quite right, but it'll do */ view japanese
2258		fd = -1;
2259		goto error;
2260	}
2261
2262	/* Now write the data into the successfully-reserved part of the file */ view japanese
2263	fd = OpenTransientFile(PGSS_TEXT_FILE, O_RDWR | O_CREAT | PG_BINARY);
2264	if (fd < 0)
2265		goto error;
2266
2267	if (pg_pwrite(fd, query, query_len, off) != query_len)
2268		goto error;
2269	if (pg_pwrite(fd, "\0", 1, off + query_len) != 1)
2270		goto error;
2271
2272	CloseTransientFile(fd);
2273
